ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Thai combo set meal specifically. FOODON_03315116 (meal food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (Composite dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a Thai rice/noodle combo set with dessert.

main_ingredients: protein (chicken, pork, shrimp, or tofu depending on selection)
beverage (soft drink or Thai iced tea)

serving.description: 1 combo set (main dish + drink + mango sticky rice dessert)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full Thai combo set meal comprising approximately 350 g of a rice or noodle main dish, 150 g of mango sticky rice dessert, and approximately 150 g of a beverage, totalling an estimated 650 g per set.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from: HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition of Foods (Pad Thai, Mango Sticky Rice, Thai Iced Tea components); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (glutinous rice, mango, coconut milk)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: This is a restaurant combo set with variable main dish selection (Pad Thai, fried rice, or other Thai noodle/rice dish). Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 350 g main dish (using HPB Pad Thai as representative anchor, ~130 kcal/100g), 150 g mango sticky rice (estimated ~180 kcal/100g from HPB and USDA components), and 150 g sweetened beverage (estimated ~40 kcal/100g). Blended per-100g values are derived from this composite. Values represent a central estimate and carry moderate uncertainty due to menu variability.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of rice-based and noodle-based Thai dishes; Pad Thai GI ~60–70 (analogous estimate); Mango sticky rice GI ~60–65; composite weighted GI estimated at 68
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific combo set. GI is estimated as a weighted composite: Pad Thai (GI ~65, University of Sydney GI Database analogous rice noodle dish), mango sticky rice (GI ~60, analogous glutinous rice with coconut milk and sugar per University of Sydney GI Database entries for glutinous rice), and sweetened beverage (GI ~65). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution across the 650 g set, the composite GI is estimated at 68 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (68 × 126.3) / 100 ≈ 85.8, where carbohydrate per serving = 20.5 g/100g × 650 g / 100 = 133.25 g; GL = 68 × 133.25 / 100 ≈ 90.6. Revised: using 20.5 g carb per 100g × 6.5 portions = 133.25 g total carb; GL = 68 × 133.25 / 100 = 90.6. Note: GL is high due to large serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce, soy sauce, and seasoning in the main dish component
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this combo set to occasional consumption, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 2015 mg per serving (310 mg/100g × 650 g) substantially exceeds the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced sauce or choosing a lower-sodium main dish variant is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving driven by glutinous rice in mango sticky rice, refined rice noodles or jasmine rice in the main dish, added sugars in coconut milk sauce, and a sweetened beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this combo set due to its very high glycemic load (estimated GL ≈ 86–91 per set), which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion reduction and substitution of the sweetened beverage with water are strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from coconut milk in mango sticky rice and potential palm oil in cooking, combined with high sodium and added sugars
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this combo set infrequently, as the combination of saturated fat from coconut milk (estimated 1.9 g/100g), high sodium, and added sugars collectively elevates cardiovascular risk; choosing a grilled protein main dish and omitting the sweetened beverage would reduce the risk profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Thai Rice or Noodle Combo Set Meal with Mango Sticky Rice is a restaurant combo set offered at Thai eateries in Singapore, such as Saap Saap Thai. The set comprises three components: (1) a customer's choice of a Thai rice or noodle main dish — commonly Pad Thai (stir-fried rice noodles with egg, bean sprouts, peanuts, and a protein such as chicken, shrimp, or tofu in a tamarind-fish sauce), Thai fried rice, or another Thai noodle preparation; (2) a refreshing beverage, typically a sweetened soft drink or Thai iced tea; and (3) mango sticky rice (khao niao mamuang) as dessert — glutinous rice steamed and served with fresh ripe mango slices and a sweetened coconut milk sauce. The overall flavour profile is a balance of savoury, sweet, tangy, and umami notes. Texturally, the set spans chewy noodles or fluffy rice, soft sticky glutinous rice, and juicy fresh mango. Nutritionally, this is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-sodium meal with significant added sugars from the dessert and beverage components. The estimated energy per set is approximately 832 kcal (128 kcal/100g × 650 g). The glycemic load is very high (estimated GL ≈ 86–91), making this meal a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content is also elevated due to fish sauce and seasoning in the main dish. Clinically, this combo set is best treated as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular meal for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions. It is a culturally representative Thai set meal format popular in Singapore's food delivery and casual dining scene.

confidence_reason: This is a restaurant combo set with a variable main dish component (customer selects from multiple Thai rice or noodle options). No single approved-source entry covers this exact composite product. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of HPB Singapore Foodo data for Pad Thai and USDA FoodData Central data for glutinous rice, mango, and coconut milk components. The GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for similar Thai rice and noodle dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are approved and defensible, but the composite introduces meaningful uncertainty.
notes: 1. This record represents a restaurant combo set (field survey data, Saap Saap Thai - Funan, SGD 20.90) with a variable main dish. The canonical food name has been normalised from the menu label 'Rice/Noodle Combo 3' to 'Thai Rice or Noodle Combo Set Meal with Mango Sticky Rice' to reflect the actual food content. 2. Serving size is estimated at 650 g based on typical Thai restaurant set portions: ~350 g main dish, ~150 g mango sticky rice, ~150 g beverage. 3. Per-100g nutrient values are composite estimates; individual component proportions may vary by restaurant and customer selection. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for Pad Thai or the USDA entries used. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component sources used. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this dish category. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for Pad Thai or the USDA entries used. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. 10. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible in Thai restaurant cooking using vegetable oils; consistent with HPB and USDA source data for similar dishes. 11. Glycemic load per serving is high (estimated 85.8–90.6) due to the large serving size and multiple high-GI carbohydrate components. 12. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Mixed' to reflect the composite nature of this set meal. 13. Halal status is not confirmed for this specific restaurant; fish sauce and potential cross-contamination mean halal flag is set to false pending verification.

Assigned FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes with gap notes. Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with consistency check. Wrote 1,387-character LLM description. Added all three health context conditions with clinical guidance. Reclassified food_group to Mixed.
